overview: This four-color Standard deck leverages a resilient elemental creature base to control the board while generating incremental advantage. By cycling through Wistfulness for card draw, Deceit for board manipulation, and Vibrance for mana acceleration, the pilot aims to outlast early pressure and close games with a superior resource count. The strategy relies on flexible interaction and steady value generation rather than explosive combos.
win_conditions: Combat damage from a wide board of elementals
Card advantage loops fueled by Wistfulness
Overwhelming mana production via Vibrance to deploy multiple threats

keep_criteria: Hands with early elemental creatures or mana dorks
Hands containing Wistfulness or Deceit for early interaction
Hands with at least three lands and a playable curve
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early plays or land drops
Hands heavy on late-game cards without acceleration
Hands missing key interaction or elemental synergies

early_game: Play lands and low-cost elementals to establish board presence
Use Deceit to disrupt opponent's early threats or bounce their creatures
Prioritize keeping the board clear while searching for Wistfulness
mid_game: Deploy Vibrance to accelerate mana and enable larger elementals
Cast Wistfulness to refill the hand and maintain pressure
Trade efficiently while preserving key removal for bigger threats
late_game: Flood the board with high-impact elementals using excess mana
Leverage card advantage to out-resource control decks
Close out games with wide attacks or targeted finishers

key_cards.name: Wistfulness
key_cards.notes: Cast when behind on cards or to enable multiple spells in a turn; prioritize keeping it in mulligans for consistency.

key_cards.name: Deceit
key_cards.notes: Use to bounce opponent's key threats, protect your elementals, or set up favorable trades; hold up for instant-speed interaction if possible.

key_cards.name: Vibrance
key_cards.notes: Play early to enable faster curve; use its abilities to pump elementals or generate extra mana for late-game sweeps.

sequencing_tips: Lead with Deceit to clear paths for early elementals before committing resources
Cast Wistfulness after resolving threats to ensure you have answers for the next turn
Use Vibrance's mana generation to smooth out four-color mana base clumps
Hold interaction until opponent commits to a play to maximize efficiency

matchups.advice: Prioritize early Deceit to bounce or remove key attackers; use Wistfulness to find answers and stabilize.
Apply steady pressure with elementals; use Vibrance to race their finishers; save Deceit for their game-winning cards.
Disrupt their key combo pieces with Deceit; use Wistfulness to draw into counters or disruption; race if they lack interaction.
